concept.concept_text: A young woman drifts through a city at night carrying invisible weight — relationships, memories, expectations. Each scene visualizes the burden through an object she encounters: a heavy door, a stacked tray, a child on her shoulders, a globe in a shop window. The chorus is the release.
concept.style_text: Neon-drenched after-rain cinematography: wet pavement reflections, sodium-yellow and electric-cyan, shallow depth of field, 35mm grain, handheld at intimate distance, locked-off at wide. Carmen Vidal meets Wong Kar-wai.

characters.name: Maya
characters.description: Mid-twenties, dark curls, oversized cream coat, scuffed black boots, gold hoop earrings, tired but resolute eyes. Carries a leather satchel.

chapters.name: Verse 1 — Weights
chapters.description: Maya moves through the city carrying objects that grow heavier each scene. The lyrics map directly to what she's holding.
chapters.style_notes: Wet pavement, sodium-yellow street light, intimate handheld framing on the objects.

scenes.name: V1 — Door
scenes.lyrics_text: Push the door, feel the weight, every hinge a hesitation
scenes.image_prompt: Medium close-up of Maya leaning her shoulder into a heavy brass-handled apartment door, breath visible in the cold air. Hallway light spills across her face. Wet street visible through the open door behind her — reflections of neon signs in pavement puddles. 35mm grain, shallow depth of field.
scenes.negative_prompt: smiling, modern phone in hand, text on signs
scenes.flow_idea: Open on the resistance of the door. The lyric is literal — show the weight of opening.
scenes.video_prompt: Maya pushes the door open one quarter of the way, pauses, then leans her full weight into it. Camera handheld, intimate distance.

lyrics_source.initial_text:
[Verse 1]
Push the door, feel the weight, every hinge a hesitation
Carry it home, carry it slow, every step a conversation

[Chorus]
Atlas, set it down
Atlas, set it down
The sky won't fall without you
Atlas, set it down
